steps.title: Match both parts of the claim
steps.content: The claim says Odalie is eager to escape the monotony of her everyday life. The right quotation must therefore show two ideas at once: that her usual life feels dull or confined, and that she wants some new experience beyond that routine. A quotation that only describes Mardi Gras, her house, or her surroundings would be incomplete.

steps.title: Eliminate partial quotations
steps.content:
• Choice A: Calls Mardi Gras tiresome after the event. That concerns the carnival, not Odalie's everyday monotony, and it undercuts the idea that she is eager for escape.
  • Choice B: Describes Odalie living in "convent-like seclusion" in the French house. It shows isolation, but not that she wants a new experience.

steps.title: Choose the complete quotation
steps.content:
• Choice D: Presents the jingle of bells, music, and songs outside Odalie's window. It describes the carnival atmosphere but does not establish her dull routine or desire to escape it.
Choice C says that life becomes dull when someone is shut up with no same-age companions and is ready for any new sensation. That gives both monotony and eagerness. The answer is C.
